segments.text: Why is Red Bull so expensive? But the can is [music] so small. See, it was not just to save money. It was actually a great design trick that changed
everything. In the 80s, [music] every drink was a fat can. If Red Bull use the same can, your brain would instantly [music] compare it to Coke. And since
Red Bull cost five times more, you would think this is just an expensive [music]
soda, so they have to look completely different. But selling less liquid for more money makes people feel cheated.
So, how do you stop them from getting [music] angry? You use the espresso effect. A thin cup of espresso cost more than a big coffee because we think small
size means [music] strong energy. The Slim Can trick does it signaled power, not thrust. You are not buying a drink, you're buying a battery. [music]
